purpose: Define an AI advisory layer that synthesizes KPI signals into scheduling recommendations using Grok 4. Advisor output is read-only intent; execution stays on existing API paths.
design_intent: AI advises, existing API executes. The advisor never writes files, triggers browser runs, or modifies scheduler state directly. All output flows through the validated /api/control-panel path.
change_impact: Adds a new precedence tier to scheduler-adaptation.policy.json between operator preset and trend multiplier. Operator always wins. Existing rule-based fallback is preserved if the AI call fails.

model.call_budget: one call per observer cycle, plus on-demand via GET /api/ai-recommendation

context_packet.description: Compact JSON snapshot assembled server-side before calling Grok 4. Targets ~800 tokens.

system_prompt.role: You are a scheduling optimizer for a forum auto-publisher bot. You receive real-time board activity and performance KPIs. Return a JSON object with your recommendation.
constraints: Never recommend stopping the scheduler entirely — that is an operator decision.
Respect the clamping bounds: intervalMinutes 5–480, gapThreshold 1–50.
If data is insufficient (confidence < 0.3), prefer conservative recommendations close to current settings.
Do not hallucinate data not present in the context packet.

recommendedIntervalMinutes.description: Recommended scheduler base interval. Jitter is applied on top by existing logic.

recommendedGapThreshold.description: Recommended minimum post gap before publishing.

reasoning.description: Plain-language explanation of the recommendation. Shown to operator in dashboard.

confidence.description: Self-assessed confidence based on signal quality.

new_endpoints.intent: Return last cached AI recommendation with its context snapshot. Read-only, no side effects.
new_endpoints.failure_mode: Returns { recommendation: null } if XAI_API_KEY absent or last call errored. Never throws.

new_endpoints.intent: Apply last cached recommendation to control-panel state. Validates bounds, records applied_at.
new_endpoints.failure_mode: 422 with reason if recommendation stale or out of bounds.

integration_with_execution_loop.trigger_point: After observe_board (step 1) completes successfully, before decide_eligibility (step 3). If advisor call fails, proceed with existing rules unchanged.
integration_with_execution_loop.scheduler_hook: recalcTrendFactor in server.ts calls buildAdvisorContext() + getAdvisorRecommendation() async. Result cached in-memory. GET /api/ai-recommendation reads cache.
integration_with_execution_loop.apply_path: POST /api/apply-ai-recommendation → validates → calls existing POST /api/control-panel internally.

safety_constraints: XAI_API_KEY absent → advisor silently disabled; no error propagation to scheduler.
Advisor call timeout 8s → skip, log warning, proceed with rules.
Output intervalMinutes and gapThreshold are clamped server-side regardless of AI output.
Operator-applied control-panel settings always override advisor cache.
Advisor recommendation is never auto-applied — operator must call POST /api/apply-ai-recommendation.

env_additions.effect: Enables Grok 4 advisor. If absent, advisor endpoints return null recommendation.
Kill-switch. Set false to disable advisor without removing the API key.
Per-call timeout in ms. Advisor skips on timeout.

kpi_decision_framework.description: How individual signals should influence the recommendation.

signals.direction: high → shorten interval; low → lengthen interval
low (<5%) → shorten interval, lower gap; high (>20%) → lengthen interval
high (>60%) → lower gap threshold; may indicate threshold is miscalibrated
high (>30%) → recommend conservative interval (don't hammer); surface in reasoning
high → use median interval, avoid extremes; penalizes confidence
current hour near daily peak → shorten interval; off-peak → lengthen
low → stay close to current settings
